scaffold-packages.notes: Scaffold only the 4 non-plugin packages: core, runtime, nx, cli. Plugin packages are created later by scaffold-plugins using the nx-generator. This is the correct order: build the tool (nx-generator), then use it to scaffold plugins (scaffold-plugins), then implement plugins.

core-types.notes: Define GeneratedSchemas, ComposedSchemas, ExportMode, and OutputPlugin types. These are the contracts all other packages depend on. Do NOT implement logic here — types only. The composeSchemas and generateSchemas functions are stubs in index.ts that the next two states fill in.

schema-extraction.notes: Parallel with schema-composition. Uses ts-morph + ts-json-schema-generator (install as deps of apigen-core). The ctx exclusion rule is name-only. Depth-limit morph fallback to 6 levels. Test fixture must cover all extraction modes + ctx filtering + optional params + Promise<T> unwrapping.

schema-composition.notes: Parallel with schema-extraction. composeSchemas only reads middleware.envelope (a plain Record) — it does NOT depend on the runtime middleware system, which is convenient. The data: {} wrapper is always present even for zero-param functions. Overrides use false to suppress a middleware field.

audit-core.notes: Verifies: generateSchemas returns correct shape for all 3 extraction modes; ctx is excluded; data: {} wrapper is always present; ts build clean; no circular imports between core packages.

runtime-middleware.notes: Parallel with runtime-dispatch. No external deps — pure TypeScript. assertNoSelfSubscription must throw ConfigurationError (a new error class defined here) on self-subscription. buildContext accumulates ctx sequentially, emits events. wireObservers wires eventMapping subscribers to the bus once at createApiPackage time.

runtime-dispatch.notes: Parallel with runtime-middleware. Three pure functions: needsEnvelopeField, dataParamNames, dispatch. dispatch is the single canonical path — all plugins use it. Test: with ctx+session (createClient path); without ctx (direct call); with zero-param function.

audit-runtime.notes: Verifies: apigen-runtime builds; recursion guard throws; override validation warns; dispatch with session middleware; no imports from apigen-core in apigen-runtime (it only needs types via the type-only import path).

scaffold-plugins.notes: Runs `nx g @adhd/apigen-nx:plugin` × 5. Depends on audit-runtime so the runtime types are stable before any plugin file is written. Depends on nx-generator for the tool itself. Generator handles tsconfig.base.json wiring automatically. --hasRun true for mcp/api-fastify/api-express; false for jsonschema/cli-output. All 5 stubs must build before proceeding.

plugin-fastify-checkpoint.notes: HUMAN GATE. Executor stops here after plugin-api-fastify passes and presents the implementation + integration test results to the plan owner. The approval file is created only after explicit human sign-off. Remaining 4 plugins do NOT start until this checkpoint passes. This catches structural issues in the reference implementation before they propagate.

plugin-jsonschema.notes: Parallel with all other plugin states. Simplest plugin — no run() method. Emits one JSON file per function at outputDir/<packageId>/<fnName>.json. Validates that the OutputPlugin interface is correct and satisfied.

plugin-mcp.notes: Parallel with other plugins. Dep: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk. Single package covers stdio + SSE + streaming-http transports via options.transport. For HTTP run() mode uses node:http built-in (no express/fastify dep). generate() emits index.ts + server.ts parameterised by transport.

plugin-api-fastify.notes: Parallel with other plugins. Dep: fastify. generate() emits routes.ts; run() spins up Fastify in-process on options.port (default 3000). Route pattern: POST /<packageId>/<fnName> body: { data: {}, ...envelope }.

plugin-api-express.notes: Parallel with other plugins. Dep: express + @types/express. Identical HTTP shape to api-fastify (POST /<id>/<fnName>, body: { data, ...envelope }), using express Router. generate() emits routes.ts.

plugin-cli-output.notes: Parallel with other plugins. Dep: commander (in the GENERATED code, not this plugin's dep). generate() emits cli.ts — Commander program with one subcommand per fn. Boolean params become flags. Optional params are not .requiredOption(). No run() method (CLI output is for generate mode only).

audit-plugins.notes: All 5 plugin packages build. OutputPlugin interface is satisfied by each. dispatch() is not duplicated (grep for inline dispatch logic). REVIEWER GATE: code-reviewer agent reviews plugin implementations before CLI states proceed.

cli-generate-cmd.notes: Parallel with cli-run-cmd and nx-generator. Wires pipeline: generateSchemas → createApiPackage → plugin.generate() → write files. generate-registry discovers packages via package.json tags. --type flag selects plugin. --opt key=value pairs populate PluginInput.options.

cli-run-cmd.notes: Parallel with cli-generate-cmd and nx-generator. run mode: import() source file at startup, pass live fns to plugin.run(). No watch/hot-reload. AbortSignal from process SIGINT forwarded to plugin. run-registry uses same registry.ts discovery logic.

nx-generator.notes: MOVED to foundation phase — parallel with schema-extraction and schema-composition. Only needs core-types (for OutputPlugin template shape). Generator: `nx g @adhd/apigen-nx:plugin --name <name> [--hasRun]` scaffolds an OutputPlugin package with correct project.json tags, tsconfig.base.json path, and conditional run() stub. Executor: shells out to npx @adhd/apigen-cli generate. Both gaps from original design fixed: (1) generator now updates tsconfig.base.json via updateJson, (2) --hasRun flag enables run() stub for server plugins.

done.notes: Terminal state. All DoD clauses confirmed. Plan complete.

audit-final-v2.notes: Final DoD audit against the v2 canonical contract. Every behavioral DoD clause proven by an audit check that drives the real entrypoint; negative controls confirmed positive. Supersedes v1 final audit.

integration-tests-v2.notes: Re-authored against the canonical contract (SPEC §15). REAL components: extractor->descriptor->harness->each transport. Export-shape matrix (named/renamed-as/default-fn/default-object/anonymous/CJS) proves F28/F29 closed. Mixed-host gateway test proves §13. real-api.ts retained as the v1 behavioral-regression fixture (dod.1/2/5/cli).

canonical-descriptor.notes: SPEC §4: Operation{id,host,namespace,path,kind,async,streaming,input,output,envelope,typeText} + Segment{raw,words}; JSON Schema 2020-12 IR. The neutral contract every extractor emits and every plugin consumes.

naming-helpers.notes: SPEC §5: @adhd/apigen-naming — tokenizer + toKebab/toPascal/toSnake/toCamel + per-transport projection (HTTP kebab, MCP _, gRPC Pascal, CLI kebab); file-name normalize; default/default-object/index rules.

ts-extractor-by-symbol.notes: SPEC §4/§14, fixes F28/F29: name operations by EXPORTED SYMBOL (honor 'as' aliases; handle default/anonymous/renamed/CJS shapes); emit canonical descriptor incl typeText; ctx-first-param excluded; data-wrapper dissolved.

audit-v2-core.notes: Export-shape matrix: named/renamed(as)/default-fn/default-object/anonymous/CJS source each yield correct descriptor ids + projections. Negative control: revert symbol-naming -> matrix goes red.

layer-harness.notes: SPEC §8: createInvoker(plugins)->invoke(op,call) composes the Layer stack around dispatch; typed-extension ctx (type-keyed, insert/read); generalizes v1 dispatch->invoke. Transports become thin adapters.

central-validation.notes: SPEC §6: built-in validation Layer — validate data vs input and envelope once before dispatch (AJV); fail -> ApiError{invalid_argument}. Replaces per-plugin/route-bound validation.

error-taxonomy.notes: SPEC §9: @adhd/apigen-errors — ApiError{code,message,details} on gRPC canonical code set + per-transport status maps (HTTP/gRPC/MCP/CLI).

audit-v2-harness.notes: Drive invoke() through REAL Layers: order preserved, ctx threaded, validation rejects bad data with invalid_argument, error codes map per transport. Negative control: remove validation Layer -> bad-data test goes red.

plugin-interface.notes: SPEC §7.1: Plugin{id,capabilities:{target,layer,mount,envelope}}; Call/Next/MountedOperation/EnvelopeCapability types; hook sugar compiles to a LayerCapability. The contract all plugins implement.

projection-transports.guard: npx --yes nx run-many -t test -p apigen-plugin-api-fastify apigen-plugin-api-express apigen-plugin-mcp apigen-plugin-cli
projection-transports.notes: SPEC §5/§7/§9: re-cast http-fastify/http-express/mcp/cli as TargetCapability plugins projecting the descriptor over the Layer harness; envelope sourced from transport metadata (strict x-<id>-*/x-adhd-*), not body; canonical projection (POST action / GET query, MCP _ join, CLI nested kebab).

logger-layer-plugin.notes: SPEC §7.2(a)/O13: convert v1 createLogger into a LayerCapability plugin (the dogfood Layer) reading typed ctx.get(Logger); per-request timing/ok/fail.

mount-plugins.guard: npx --yes nx run-many -t test -p apigen-openapi apigen-plugin-openapi apigen-plugin-health
mount-plugins.notes: SPEC §7.2(b,c)/§12: MountCapability plugins. @adhd/apigen-openapi = neutral descriptor->OpenAPI content (common); thin per-host openapi + health mount plugins add _meta/openapi and _meta/health operations that flow through the harness.

audit-v2-projection.notes: Real servers: each transport returns ground-truth values; envelope rides metadata; logger Layer wraps; openapi/health mounts answer. Negative control: drop a route from a generated server -> tools/list/route set no longer deep-equals derived ground truth -> red.

package-restructure.guard: npx --yes nx run-many -t build -p apigen-core apigen-naming apigen-errors apigen-schema apigen-conformance apigen-gateway
package-restructure.notes: SPEC §12: split COMMON (core/naming/errors/schema/conformance/gateway + neutral codegen/*) vs PER-LANGUAGE ts/* (-core,-extractor,-runtime,plugins as apigen-ts-plugin-*). Rule: descriptor-in/artifact-out=common; touches host fns/runtime=per-language.

unified-cli.notes: SPEC §13: the ONE adhd-apigen orchestrator — detect lang per source, drive per-lang extractor subprocess, merge into one descriptor, generate/run; --type <plugin> (target) and --use <plugin> (layer/mount/envelope; pkg specifier OR local path).

gateway.notes: SPEC §13: @adhd/apigen-gateway — sidecar topology for mixed-host run (spawn per-language runtime, route each op to its owning runtime over local IPC, present one transport); single in-process fast path when all one host.

conformance-vectors.notes: SPEC §12/§14: @adhd/apigen-conformance — cross-language conformance vectors every host runtime must pass (descriptor round-trip, naming projection, validation, error mapping); codifies the host-SDK/extractor contract.

scaffold-v2-common.guard: npx --yes nx run-many -t build -p apigen-naming apigen-errors apigen-schema apigen-conformance apigen-gateway apigen-codegen-openapi
scaffold-v2-common.notes: SPEC §12: scaffold the 6 COMMON packages in their final homes with full nx wiring (tsconfig.base paths, vite.config, tsconfig.lib/spec, layer:logic/platform:shared tags) — mirrors v1 scaffold-packages. Fixes R3 (unscaffolded pkgs) + R6 (final homes → package-restructure becomes a verify gate). apigen-schema gets its owning state here.

scaffold-v2-ts-plugins.guard: npx --yes nx run-many -t build -p apigen-ts-plugin-logger apigen-ts-plugin-openapi apigen-ts-plugin-health
scaffold-v2-ts-plugins.notes: SPEC §12: scaffold the 3 new TS plugin packages (logger=layer, openapi+health=mount) in final ts/plugins/* homes with nx wiring; platform:node. Fixes R3.

class-exports.notes: SPEC §10 (H2=now): static methods -> ops at path=[file,class,method] now; instances OPT-IN now: kind:constructor (POST .../class -> {instanceId}) + kind:instance-method dispatch via instanceId + registry with TTL/dispose lifecycle. Stateful caveat documented (no horizontal scale without sticky/external store).

streaming-projection.guard: npx --yes nx run-many -t test -p apigen-plugin-mcp apigen-plugin-api-fastify apigen-runtime
streaming-projection.notes: SPEC §11 (D6 full streaming now): streaming:true -> async stream; Layer stream-lifecycle (start/each/end/error) per §8.1; consumer-pull backpressure; signal cancellation runs the end path; error-after-first-chunk delivered in-band per the §11 table (SSE event:error / gRPC trailing status / MCP progressive error / CLI stderr+exit) adopting Connect's semantics.

python-host.notes: SPEC §14 (H1): minimal REAL second host so the §13.1 sidecar-gateway IPC + partial-availability are proven against a true foreign runtime, not a TS stub (fixes R9). Python -extractor (source->descriptor), -runtime (invoke/validate/dispatch), one echo plugin, gateway IPC adapter. Must pass apigen-conformance vectors.
